finding: check_run labeled the round 1->2 transition a confirmation transition and SKIPPED RUN-003 disposition-coverage for round 1's 2 medium + 5 low findings -- but 1->2 was a REAL revision (revision-response.round-1.md exists, 7/7 applied). Reproduced: `check_run.py --handoff handoff` prints 'round 1 -> 2 is a confirmation transition ... requirements skipped'. Misfired on two loose signals keyed to next_round=2: edit-log.round-2.md l8 'triggers a CONFIRMATION round (round 3)' (CONFIRMATION_PHRASE_RE, first-40-lines) and score-history's round-2 row 'First clean -> confirmation trigger' (signal-3 substring). Both describe what a CLEAN round triggers NEXT, not that round 2 IS a confirmation. No false-pass this run (dispositions all exist; RUN-004 id-continuity still ran), but the heuristic would mask a genuine RUN-003 disposition gap in any future run where a truly-revised round's log or score-row merely mentions the word 'confirmation'. Inverse failure mode of the applied 2026-07-03-check-run-confirmation-round-model (that fixed the model UNDER-firing / false RUN-001 FAIL on a real confirmation round; this is the model OVER-firing / falsely skipping a real revision's checks).
recommendation: New proposal 2026-07-06-check-run-confirmation-transition-veto: make the presence of revision-response.round-N.md dispositive -- veto the confirmation classification at the N->N+1 transition whenever that file exists (a confirmation round takes no revision by spec, so a revision-response cannot coexist with a real confirmation transition), forcing RUN-003 to run. Strict refinement: a legitimate confirmation round has no revision-response, so the veto never fires on it (verified: revision-response.round-2.md absent, so the real 2->3 confirmation stays correctly classified). Marker-tightening is optional defense-in-depth but must keep the loose fallback to avoid regressing un-marked legitimate confirmation rounds. propose-only; gate unpatched.
